note_markdown:
# Alphabet Q3 2026 Earnings Note

Alphabet reported consolidated revenue of $102.7 billion [F2] for Q3 2026, missing consensus by $1.7 billion [C1]. EPS (diluted) came in at $2.15 [F1].

Management strengthened its Search AI commentary. "AI-driven features in Search, including AI Overviews and AI Mode, continue to deliver strong engagement" [L1]

Brian Nowak from Morgan Stanley pressed on the capex step-up. "The increase reflects stronger than expected Cloud demand pull-through, expanded build for our internal Gemini training fleet, and selective infrastructure pre-positioning. Cloud RPO grew 38 percent year over year." [Q1]

Management formalised the capex commitment for fiscal 2026. "we now expect full-year 2026 capex to be approximately 88 billion dollars" [K1]

diffs.current_text: AI-driven features in Search, including AI Overviews and AI Mode, continue to deliver strong engagement
diffs.prior_text: AI-driven features in Search continue to show solid engagement

qa_pairs.question_text: Can you walk through what is driving the capex increase to 88 billion, and how confident you are in the demand signals?
qa_pairs.answer_text: The increase reflects stronger than expected Cloud demand pull-through, expanded build for our internal Gemini training fleet, and selective infrastructure pre-positioning. Cloud RPO grew 38 percent year over year.

commitments.commitment_text: Full-year 2026 capex guidance raised to approximately $88 billion
commitments.source_quote: we now expect full-year 2026 capex to be approximately 88 billion dollars

note: deterministic critic may not fire on direction-only contradictions; this perturbation targets the LLM critic in Phase 5c.
